provenance.source_corpus: Avian recolonization of eastern Canadian bogs after peat mining

source_spans.text: vacuum peat mining removes all live bog vegetation
abandoned sites, especially post vacuum, were less vegetated
Bird species richness and abundance were similar in natural and post-blockcut sites and both were higher than in post-vacuum sites
Bird communities were closely associated with vegetation structure

natural return of vegetation after several years of recovery following exploitation using current methods is incomplete and not sufficient to elicit the return of bird species assemblages typical of natural peatlands
